titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Charles Blum Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Tawny Ryan Nelb and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 21, 2020 at 07:56
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: The papers consist of letters, many written by Paul Blum to his family during his service in the U. S. Army Ambulance Service with the French army, 1918-1919. Further correspondence with numerous associates through the time of his death in 1981 is included, as well as memorabilia, a biography, and printed material.

langmaterial.language: The papers are in English, French, and German.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Paul Blum, 1976, and of Robert S. Greene, 1993-1995, 1999, 2005 and 2007.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Charles Blum Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Charles Blum was born on March 31, 1898, in Yokohama, Japan, and spent his childhood in Japan, Switzerland, France, and New York City. Blum was a private in the Yale R.O.T.C. and enlisted in the U. S. Ambulance Service in 1917. He was sent overseas in 1918, and served with the French army from March 1918 to March 1919. Blum was awarded the Croix de Guerre on August 12, 1918. He returned to the United States in 1919 and graduated from the Sheffield Scientific School, Yale University, in 1921. Following graduation, Blum was a literary agent and free-lance writer. He joined the Foreign Service during World War II, remaining until 1954. Blum retired to Japan, where he lived until his death in 1981.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of letters, many written by Paul Blum to his family during his service in the U. S. Army Ambulance Service with the French army, 1918-1919. Further correspondence with numerous associates through the time of his death in 1981 is included, as well as memorabilia, a biography, and printed material.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Robert S. Greene in this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: (88 ALS, 12 ACS, 1 ANS, 3 TL) 1918-1919, n.d.
ALS, ACS, TL Paul Blum to his mother [Rose (Isaacs) Blum, 73 items]
ALS, ACS Paul Blum to his father [Henri Blum, 4 items]
ALS, ACS Paul Blum to an aunt [Mrs. Max Blum?, 8 items]
ALS Paul Blum to an uncle
ALS Paul Blum to a cousin Jean [3 items]
ALS Paul Blum and an unidentified party
ANS Paul Blum to steamship company
ALS Jean [?] to Uncle Henri [Blum] and Aunt Rose [Blum]
TL John J. Pershing to "My Fellow Soldiers"
ALS Mrs. Henri Blum to Paul Blum
[Letters badly damaged by fire, approximately 3 items]
TL selected typescript of above correspondence
"Der Section Zeitung," a newsletter about the SSU [Section Sanitaire Americaine] 583
List of names and addresses, presumably of the SSU 583

Miscellaneous memorabilia: including coin vouchers, carte d'identité, invitation to a ball, large poster "Announcement of the Peoples Government" [1918 Nov 13, removed to War Poster Collection], Christmas package coupon
Passes for leave: 4 ADS, 6 TDS, 3 DS [13 items]
List of names of the 578 U.S. Ambulance Service to be discharged
Correspondence and documents pertaining to naturalization and citizenship
"My Trip": Blum's diary of his return to France
"Iowa City to the Hindenburg Line," Annals of Iowa

title.part: The History of the United States Army Ambulance Service: with the French and Italian Armies, 1917-1919

did.unittitle: United States Army Ambulance Service Association: certificate and identification card
Croix de Guerre (3) and military ribbons (3)
Blum, Henri (father of Paul C. Blum)
Personal papers [includes birth and marriage certificates, will, work papers, carnet militaire]
Blum, Rose (Isaacs) (mother of Paul C. Blum)
Dulles, Allen W. [includes translation by Paul Blum of "The Dulles Operation," chapter 22 of A History of the End of the War ; much of the chapter was based on an article by Fujimara]
Yoshida, Shigeru [Japan's first post-war prime minister]; includes clippings
Notes [departure from La Croix en Brie]
Personal papers [includes academic record, identity cards, carnet militaire]
Dulles, Allen [ The Secret Surrender ; inscribed in front cover to Paul Blum]
Fujimara, Yoshiro [biographical information, translation of peace negotiations with Allen Dulles]
German surrender [meeting with German representatives re possible surrender on Italian front; P.C.B.'s handwritten notes; typed report to Allen Dulles]
Yale University [gift of hyakumanto and dharani to the Yale University Library in memory of Norman Holmes Pearson; includes correspondence, photographs, Yale Alumni Magazine article, 1976 Mar]
"The Bell at Shinagawa-Ji," Japan Quarterly [includes typescript and correspondence]
"Father Mounicou's Bakamatsu Diary," Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan [translated with an introduction by Paul C. Blum]
"Haneda Airport, Japan's Port of Entry from the Air," Radio Japan
No. 1 [Colombia (N.Y. to Cucuta), pp. 1-219; papers laid in]
No. 2 [Venezuela and the Guianas (San Cristobal to Para), pp. 220-391; papers laid in]
No. 3 [Brazil and Paraguay (Para to Buenos Aires), pp. 392-565; papers laid in]
No. 4 [Argentina, Falkland Islands, Chile and Peru (Buenos Aires to Peru), pp. 556-713; paper laid in]
No. 5 [Peru and Bolivia (Lima to Cochabamba), pp. 714-984; paper laid in]
No. 6 [Bolivia (Cochabamba to Cobija), pp. 895-1044; papers laid in]
No. 7 [U.S.A., Panama, and Hawaii (Pittsburgh to Yokohama), pp. 1045-1201]
No. 8 [Japan, Korea, Manchuria (Yokohama to Dairen), pp. 1202-1360; paper laid in]
No. 9 [China, Indo-China, and Siam (Dairen to Singapore), pp. 1361-1502; papers laid in]
No. 10 [Borneo and Java (Singapore to Bali), pp. 1503-1680; papers laid in]
No. 11 [Bali, Lombok, Sunda Islands, Moluccas, and Celebes, pp. 1691-1895; papers laid in]
No. 12 [Torres Straits to Zanzibar (Australia and the East Coast of Africa), pp. 1896-2128]
No. 13 [Zanzibar to Algiers, via Leopoldville and Lagos, pp. 2129-2488; papers laid in]
The Paul C. Blum Collection: A Catalogue of Books , Yokohama Archives of History
"Pies for the Ambulance," Yale Literary Magazine
Blum-San! Scholar, Soldier, Gentleman, Spy: The Many Lives of Paul Blum by Robert S. Greene [2 copies]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession is organized chronologically as received. It is comprised of correspondence between Paul Blum and numerous associates, among whom his cousin Robert (Bob) Greene, the artist Claude Remusat, colleague George (Jack) H. Kerr, and sister Mrs. Marguerite (Maggie) Amdur, figure prominently. The correspondence from 1940 contains reproductions of Blum's notes taken during the German Blitzkrieg into France in June. Several letters from Allen Dulles, 1952 and 1967-1968, briefly consider the historical record of their work in Japan at the end of World War II. Letters of condolence addressed to Greene and Kerr, following Blum's death in August of 1981, fill three folders. English transcriptions of Blum's letters to Mrs. Iwanami, his functionary in Japan, and copies of his extended correspondence with the author R. Gordon Wasson follow the general correspondence. Blum's passports, 1926-1979, and personal documents conclude the accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession comprises an early edited typescript draft of Robert S. Greene's Blum-san, a biography of Paul Blum, and transcriptions of Greene's interviews with various Blum associates done as part of his research for the book.

did.unittitle: Sketch of Paul Blum in St. Jean le Thomas by Claude Rémusat (photocopy)

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.
